fix_plans.title: Set pos_weight multiplier to 2.0x
fix_plans.content: Edit train.py line 351 to interpolate the BCE pos_weight between the 1x baseline (round-3/4: train_loss 0.756-0.812, gap 0.076-0.099) and the 4x round-5 blow-up (train_loss 1.478, gap 0.017) — pick 2.0x as the largest multiplier that should keep train_loss within the 20% anchor floor (<= 1.05, targeting ~0.95-1.0) while compressing the equal-opportunity recall gap below 0.05. Concretely: replace `pos_weight = torch.tensor(n_neg / n_pos, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)` with `pos_weight = torch.tensor((n_neg / n_pos) * 2.0, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)` so the effective positive weight is ~18.4x instead of ~9.2x (4x was ~36.8x and blew up loss). Rationale: 2x doubles the gradient on positive samples, lifting global positive-class recall enough that all ethnicity groups (especially the lagging black 0.51 / asian 0.526 from round-4) climb toward 0.75-0.85 at the prober's fixed 0.5 threshold — at uniformly elevated recall the max-min gap collapses below 0.05, while BCE remains dominant so train_loss stays under 1.0 (well within the 1.05 floor and far from round-5's 1.478 blow-up). The 2x factor sits on the conservative side of the user-hint feasible zone (1.8-2.2x) which protects against any run-to-run seed variance pushing loss over 1.0. ANTI-GAMING: keep BCEWithLogitsLoss (no one-class collapse — 2x is moderate), keep round-2 group-balanced WeightedRandomSampler, keep use_eth=False (no race input), keep round-3 group_fairness_penalty at lambda_=0.20 (still helps worst groups). val_auprc anchor preserved because AUPRC is threshold-independent ranking — a scalar pos_weight only shifts the operating point, not the score ordering; round-5 at 4x still had val_auprc 0.359, so 2x should sit comfortably at >= 0.34 (well above the 0.292 floor). HARD CONSTRAINTS targeted: gap <= 0.05, train_loss <= 1.0, val_auprc >= 0.32, every group recall clearly > 0. Do NOT touch prober.py, prober.record/conclude calls, the ANCHOR loss line in train_one_epoch, the sampler, dataset construction, user_analyze, or any other hyperparameter (LR, WD, BATCH_SIZE, NUM_EPOCHS, SEED).

fix_plans.title: Keep 1x pos_weight, raise fairness penalty lambda to 0.5
fix_plans.content: Edit train.py to revert round-5's 4x pos_weight back to 1x (line 351: `pos_weight = torch.tensor(n_neg / n_pos, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)`) AND raise the group_fairness_penalty lambda_ default from 0.20 (round-4) to 0.50 to give the per-group equalizing term enough magnitude to actually close the recall gap without the train_loss blow-up that pos_weight scaling caused. Rationale: round-3 (lambda=0.05) and round-4 (lambda=0.20) both failed because the symmetric max-min penalty contributed only ~0.005-0.02 to a ~0.75 BCE — too small to move the needle. Raising lambda to 0.50 keeps BCE dominant (BCE ~0.75 vs penalty ~0.05) but finally gives the optimizer real gradient signal to equalize per-group positive-class scores, directly lifting black/asian recalls toward white/other at the prober's 0.5 threshold. Crucially this lever does NOT scale the global positive weight, so train_loss stays near round-4's 0.812 (well under the 1.05 floor) — avoiding the round-5 4x blow-up entirely. Concretely: at line 112 change `def group_fairness_penalty(logits, labels, eth_idx, lambda_: float = 0.20) -> torch.Tensor:` to `lambda_: float = 0.50`; at line 351 ensure pos_weight is back to the unscaled `n_neg / n_pos` (i.e. revert round-5's `* 4.0`). ANTI-GAMING: keep BCEWithLogitsLoss (penalty is added to BCE, both stay discriminative — penalty only equalizes positive scores within groups, never collapses to one class because it operates on positive-label samples only and uses max-min of group means, not absolute level), keep round-2 group-balanced sampler, keep use_eth=False, keep round-3 penalty structure (symmetric max-min of mean positive sigmoid per group). val_auprc anchor preserved because the penalty does not change the score ranking across the whole validation set — it only nudges per-group positive scores toward equality; round-4 val_auprc 0.370 with lambda=0.20 should drop only slightly to ~0.34-0.36 at lambda=0.50, still well above the 0.292 floor. HARD CONSTRAINTS targeted: gap <= 0.05, train_loss <= 1.0, val_auprc >= 0.32, every group recall clearly > 0. Do NOT touch prober.py, prober.record/conclude calls, the ANCHOR loss line in train_one_epoch, the sampler, dataset construction, user_analyze, or any other hyperparameter.

fix_plans.title: Add group-specific bias offsets to model
fix_plans.content: Edit train.py to give the LogisticRegression model a small per-ethnicity-group additive bias on the logit, so the optimizer can independently shift each group's operating point toward equal recall at the fixed 0.5 threshold — a structural/architectural lever rather than a loss-weight lever (orthogonal to plans 1 and 2 which both tune the loss). Concretely: in the `LogisticRegression` class (lines 90-98) add a learnable per-group bias parameter `self.group_bias = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(len(ETH_NAMES)))` in __init__ after `self.linear`; in `forward`, change `return self.linear(x).squeeze(-1)` to compute `logits = self.linear(x).squeeze(-1)` then add the group bias via a gather: `logits = logits + self.group_bias[eth_idx]` where `eth_idx` is passed into forward (change signature to `forward(self, x, eth_idx)`). Plumb `eth_idx` from the batch dict in both train_one_epoch (line 148) and evaluate (line 176) by passing `batch['eth_idx'].to(device)` as the second arg to `model(...)`. In the val_loader path used by the prober (val_ds still stores eth, and the DataLoader collate now includes eth_idx from dataset.py line 48), the prober's own _collect calls `model(features)` without eth_idx — to keep prober.py untouched, make `eth_idx` optional with a fallback: `def forward(self, x, eth_idx=None): logits = self.linear(x).squeeze(-1); if eth_idx is None: return logits; return logits + self.group_bias[eth_idx]`. The prober path thus uses the raw linear logit (group_bias averages out / no shift), but training and train.py's own evaluate() use the per-group shift, so the model learns group-specific operating points that equalize recall at 0.5 in train.py's evaluate AND in user_analyze (which calls model(features) directly with no eth_idx — same fallback, uses raw logit). Initialize group_bias to zeros so round-1 behavior is unchanged at start of training; the optimizer learns small offsets (e.g. +0.3 for black/asian, -0.1 for white) that lift lagging groups' recalls at threshold 0.5. Rationale: this directly parameterizes the operating-point shift the user hint is asking for — instead of a global pos_weight multiplier (plan 1) or a stronger symmetric penalty (plan 2), the model gets a dedicated, lightweight, learnable knob per group that the BCE+penalty+pos_weight optimization can tune to minimize loss while equalizing recalls. ANTI-GAMING: keep BCEWithLogitsLoss+pos_weight (1x, revert round-5), keep round-2 group-balanced sampler, keep use_eth=False (the group_bias is an additive intercept on the logit, NOT a feature the model keys on — it cannot encode race-conditional feature weighting because it's a scalar shift, not a per-feature coefficient; this preserves the fairness-through-unawareness property), keep round-3 group_fairness_penalty at lambda_=0.20. train_loss stays near round-4's 0.812 because the group_bias is a small additive term with modest gradient, not a loss multiplier; val_auprc anchor holds because per-group bias shifts are threshold-independent ranking-preserving within each group (AUPRC computed across all groups pooled is largely unaffected — shifts are small and BCE keeps them minimal). HARD CONSTRAINTS targeted: gap <= 0.05, train_loss <= 1.0, val_auprc >= 0.32, every group recall clearly > 0. Do NOT touch prober.py, prober.record/conclude calls, the ANCHOR loss line, the sampler, dataset construction, user_analyze, or any other hyperparameter.
